questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt:
With reference to the Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET), consider the following statements:
1. It was an initiative introduced by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
2. It aimed to provide a uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools.
3. The framework was recently scrapped due to the reluctance of procurers to sign Power Sale Agreements.

How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: URET was associated with the Ministry of Power, not the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). Statement 2 is correct: It aimed to provide a uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools. Statement 3 is correct: It was scrapped because procurers were reluctant to sign Power Sale Agreements due to uncertainty of tariffs over a three-year fixed period, which caused stalled projects.
questions.takeaway: The URET framework by the Ministry of Power aimed to average competitive bid tariffs of similar RE projects in central pools but was scrapped due to procurer reluctance to sign Power Sale Agreements amid tariff uncertainty.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET) scrapped by Ministry of Power. Aims to provide uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools. Reasons for revocation: Procurers were reluctant to sign Power Sale Agreements due to uncertainty of tariffs over the three-year fixed period causing stalled projects.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Which one of the following best describes the mechanism proposed under the recently scrapped Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET) framework?

options.text: It aimed to provide a uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools.
It mandated a single, fixed national tariff for all renewable energy generation regardless of the bidding process.
It provided direct viability gap funding to distribution companies to offset the high capital costs of renewable energy.
It pooled all conventional and renewable energy tariffs to arrive at a blended national average cost for consumers.

questions.explanation: The URET framework was designed to provide a uniform tariff to consumers by averaging the competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools, rather than fixing a single national tariff arbitrarily or blending it with conventional energy.
questions.takeaway: URET's core mechanism was the averaging of competitively bid tariffs within central pools to standardize costs for consumers.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Aims to provide uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools.
